summary: A Ukrainian drone strike hit the middle superstructure of the Russian frigate "Admiral Essen" in Novorossiysk Bay, detonating PK-10 countermeasure grenade launchers and damaging the TK-25 electronic warfare complex. Russian air defense units, specifically the Rubicon Center, reported intercepting over 1,500 Ukrainian fixed-wing UAVs, with 75% belonging to just 11 models, including 343 Leleka-100 drones. Ukrainian forces conducted FPV strikes 50+ km into occupied Donetsk, targeting military and logistics vehicles, while Russian operators used ZALA Lancet loitering munitions to hit group targets despite electronic warfare interference. Ukrainian company Vyriy developed new "Lightning" UAV versions with alternative control systems and retargeting capabilities. Russian forces are now deploying fixed-wing UAVs carrying two FPV drones each, prompting calls for enhanced anti-drone measures including detectors, shotguns, and Antidronin capsules. Russian operators destroyed 107 Ukrainian targets on March 6, 2026, across multiple directions including Donbas and Sumy. A FAB-3000 bomb strike with UMPK on Ukrainian positions in Krasny Liman was captured via optical fiber FPV drone "KVN." Analysts noted that Ukrainian innovations like FPV drones and Lancet systems are quickly adopted and scaled by Russian forces due to lack of centralized Ukrainian systems for collecting and scaling successful practices.
